title: Boat Rental in Victoria Bc: Costs, Seasons and Tips for 2026
html:
Planning a self-drive adventure from Victoria's Inner Harbour typically costs from €150 for a two-hour rental of a small motorboat. BoatHire24 connects you with verified owners across the region, making it simple to compare options and prices. Whether you want to explore the Gulf Islands or just cruise past Fisherman's Wharf, the platform provides a reliable starting point.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Victoria Bc?

A shared group tour starts around €60 per person, while a private motorboat for half a day costs from €250. Prices vary with boat size, duration and season. A full-day catamaran charter for up to 12 people typically runs between €800 and €1,200. Crewed luxury yachts can exceed €3,000 for a 24-hour booking.

  • Shared tour (2 hours): from €60 per person
  • Small motorboat (half-day): from €250
  • Catamaran (full-day, 12 guests): €800, €1,200
  • Crewed luxury yacht (24 hours): from €3,000

Where to set off in Victoria Bc

The Inner Harbour is the most central launch point, with several marinas offering short-term berthing. Fisherman's Wharf provides a colourful, floating community and easy access to the Gorge Waterway. For longer trips, head to Oak Bay Marina or the Victoria International Ferry Terminal at Swartz Bay. From any of these points it is about 8 nautical miles to the southern tip of Salt Spring Island, a popular day-cruise destination. The waters around Chatham and Discovery Islands are also well worth exploring.

Which boat to rent

  1. Axopar 28ft, ideal for fast coastal hops and exploring coves; 8 guests; from €350 for a half-day. For this specific model, check the Limpopo listing on BoatHire24 for real availability and prices.
  2. Lagoon 42 catamaran, stable and spacious for a full-day family cruise; 12 guests; from €900.
  3. Centre-console fishing boat 22ft, best for angling trips in the strait; 6 guests; from €280 for half a day.
  4. Azimut 50 flybridge, a luxurious choice for a crewed sunset charter; 10 guests; from €1,500 for 4 hours.
  5. Boston Whaler 19ft, easy to handle for first-timers; 5 guests; from €180 for two hours.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Victoria Bc?

In British Columbia, anyone born after 5 April 1989 must hold a Pleasure Craft Operator Card. For foreign visitors, the operator will confirm whether a temporary licence or an equivalency is acceptable. It is always best to check with the boat owner before booking.

When cruising near Race Rocks, keep at least 200 metres from the lighthouse and the resident sea lions, the tidal currents there can reach 4 knots.

At a glance

  • Price band: €60, €3,000 per booking
  • Peak season: June to September
  • Typical trip length: 2 hours to full day
  • Max guest capacity (without crew): 12
  • Currency: EUR (prices quoted throughout)

Questions about boat rental in Victoria Bc

Can I rent a boat without any previous experience?

Yes, many operators offer a short briefing and a trial run before you set off, especially for smaller boats with outboard engines.

What is the best time of year for a boat rental in Victoria Bc?

July and August have the calmest seas and warmest weather, though June and September also offer good conditions with fewer crowds.

Are there any restricted areas I should avoid?

Parts of the Gorge Waterway have speed limits, and Race Rocks Ecological Reserve is off-limits to all vessels; stick to marked channels.

Can I book a boat last minute in Victoria Bc?

It is possible in shoulder months, but during July and August advance booking at least a week ahead is recommended to secure your preferred boat.

To find the right vessel for your trip, use boat hire Victoria Bc on BoatHire24 to compare verified listings, real-time availability and transparent prices. If you own a boat in the region, you can also list it on the marketplace and start earning from your local knowledge.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Victoria Bc and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
